title: Upcoming Hurricanes
alt: I'd like to see more damage assessments for hurricanes hitting New York and flooding Manhattan -- something like the 1938 Long Island Express, but aimed a bit more to the west.  It's just a matter of time.
transcript:
[An unlabelled map shows the region roughly between central Canada and northernmost parts of South America. Dotted lines indicating hurricane paths cover the map, all red except Hurricane Blue, which is blue. Each line is labelled - here follows the labels as they appear from the top and down:]
Hurricane Where-the-Hell-Is-Bermuda
Hurricane Illinois-Has-It-Too-Easy
Hurricane Freud
Hurricane Screw-It-Let's-Just-Trash-Florida-Again
Hurricane Red
Hurricane Blue
Hurricane cos(x)
explanation:
This comic gives ludicrous and ironic upcoming  paths on an unlabelled map of the  that shows the region roughly between central  and northern . Blue and red dotted lines indicate the future hurricane paths.

===Hurricanes===
====Hurricane Where-The-Hell-Is-Bermuda====
Enters from the east side of the map, wanders around the  in a scribble that seems to take the shape of an . Then it goes north for a while, and then peters out without entering the . The Bermuda Triangle is a location in the Atlantic Ocean loosely framed by the three corners , , and . The myth is that (too) many ships and planes get lost once they enter inside the area of this triangle and disappear without a trace. In this case, the hurricane gets lost before entering and can't even find the triangle. It may also simply be a reference to the statistic that Bermuda is affected by , and that this hurricane got lost on its way to its target.

====Hurricane Illinois-Has-It-Too-Easy====
Comes from somewhere to the north-west, goes through , and then back to the north-west. Illinois is located far from the ocean, and thus suffers few hurricanes - this particular one is extremely unlikely, and according to the name, exists purely so that Illinois will have a hurricane to deal with. Interestingly enough (though it did not affect the Chicago area or correspond with the path displayed in the comic), roughly one year later, a , a storm resembling a hurricane or tropical storm in movement and form,  central and South Illinois, in addition to much of  and . Also, while it had never reached hurricane strength, 's center tracked over Illinois (and then Iowa) before becoming extratropical, and other tropical and post-tropical cycles have affected Illinois. For example, while its center did not pass over Illinois,  dropped over ten inches of rain in some parts of Illinois before becoming extratropical, flooding highways there for three days.

====Hurricane Freud====
Refers to , who believed that accidental sexual expression was a reflection of the unconscious mind's sexual desires. The hurricane's path forms a pair of testicles beside Florida. Florida, due to its shape and location, resembles a penis, and the hurricane's shape and position exemplify Freud's ideas.

====Hurricane Screw-It-Let's-Just-Trash-Florida-Again====
Comes from the east, starts to curve to the north, and then turns sharply to head straight for Florida and zigzag through it four times before dying out. Sticking out from the rest of the US, Florida is prone to hurricanes from the East, South, and West. And with the state not being very high or wide, it is common for a hurricane to run over Florida, lose some strength, then rebuild strength over the hot waters in the Gulf of Mexico, only to do a U-turn and strike again. This is not exactly what happens with this particular hurricane, where it turns out into the Atlantic Ocean again each time, suggesting a malicious intent.

====Hurricane Red and Hurricane Blue====
Blue is the only hurricane path drawn in blue. The two hurricanes are playing a game zipping in straight lines and right angles around , , and . When Red successfully cuts off Blue, the latter instantly dies, and then Red dies shortly thereafter. The game they play is the game of  from the  based on the movie . Hurricane Blue lost because it crashed into the wall of light left by Hurricane Red's . (Note that real hurricanes are not dotted lines; the two hurricanes would have merged long before Hurricane Blue "lost.")

====Hurricane cos(x)====
Forms a curve in the shape of a  above the bottom edge of the map. Its path resembles a  wave. This kind of  can, however, both be expressed as sin(x) or cos(x), the latter being a . They look exactly the same when there is no clearly defined coordinate system as in this case. Given this oscillates between six and ten degrees north (using Paramaribo and the southern shore of 
Trinidad as references) perhaps this hurricane might be better described as Hurricane 2 cos (x) + 8.

===Title Text===
The title text refers to the  (also known as the Long Island Express) that caused $4.7 billion in damage. Had it been further west, it could have caused more damage, as the right side of a hurricane is stronger and more destructive than the left side, as the winds on the right side push water inland. Randall asks for more damage assessments for such a hurricane that would be able to flood  in . Only four years after this cartoon was published, making it almost prophetic,  did strike the  as a  storm. Hurricane Sandy caused an estimated $74 billion in damage. 

The 1938 hurricane is also referenced in 980: Money, where it is calculated that it would have caused $78 billion had it happened in 2011. However, if that hurricane had taken the same turn as Sandy did, the cost today could have been a staggering $237 billion.
